title: Attractor
embodies: Sixteen texts are each recursively trained on their own output (order-6 character model, deterministic seed) and their collapse is measured against a single controlled variable: the source's own tail density (distinct 6-grams / length). The work runs the loop live in the browser and finds three things, all verified over six seeds. (1) A threshold-and-saturation law: below tail density ~0.06 self-training does not destroy but ACCRETES structure toward a floor (collapse magnitude < 0); above ~0.35 it saturates near 0.80 regardless of how rich the source was. (2) A shared attractor: sources spanning a 70x range in starting richness (20 to 1438 distinct 6-grams) converge to a narrow floor band decoupled from where they began. (3) Re-opening the exit: pure replacement collapses ~78%, but keeping the real data in the loop (accumulation) loses only ~40% and holds far more of the tail open. Grounds the F-030 tail hypothesis in Dohmatob et al. (A Tale of Tails, ICML 2024) and the accumulation remedy in Gerstgrasser et al. (COLM 2024). Honest limit: the floor's absolute value depends on the fixed L and k; and the interesting mid-curve is evidenced only by synthetic sources — the real anchors test only the saturated plateau.
